# Domino Majlis PRO Constitution
## Article 01 — Project Mission

---

# Official Name

Domino Majlis PRO

---

# Purpose

Domino Majlis PRO is a professional real-world domino match recording,
competition management,
identity,
ranking,
statistics,
achievement,
and community platform.

It is NOT a video game.

---

# Core Philosophy

The application represents real domino events.

The application does not simulate gameplay.

The application records and manages real matches that have already occurred.

Every engineering decision shall preserve this principle.

---

# Primary Objectives

The system must always prioritize:

1. Stability

2. Correctness

3. Data Integrity

4. Identity Integrity

5. User Trust

6. Maintainability

7. Performance

8. Scalability

New features must never compromise these objectives.

---

# Engineering Philosophy

Small safe modifications are preferred over large rewrites.

Existing approved architecture must be preserved.

Every modification should have the minimum possible impact.

Avoid introducing technical debt.

---

# Architectural Goals

Maintain:

• Shell Navigation

• MVVM

• Service-oriented architecture

• Dependency Injection

• Strong separation of concerns

• Reusable UI components

• Modular services

• Modular models

---

# User Experience Philosophy

Professional.

Simple.

Predictable.

Responsive.

Minimal user effort.

No unnecessary dialogs.

No unnecessary page redesign.

Premium visual quality.

---

# Data Philosophy

Data correctness is more important than visual effects.

Never lose user data.

Never silently overwrite user information.

Never change identity based on display names.

IDs are authoritative.

---

# Identity Philosophy

Developer is unique.

Founder is limited.

Honor is honorary.

Normal users cannot access developer tools.

Identity synchronization must remain consistent across the entire application.

---

# AI Engineering Principles

Before modifying code the AI shall:

Understand the task.

Understand affected architecture.

Locate dependencies.

Estimate impact.

Choose the safest implementation.

Build.

Verify.

Report.

Never skip analysis.

Never guess architecture.

Never redesign approved systems without explicit instruction.

---

# Completion Rules

A task is considered complete only when:

The implementation is finished.

The solution builds successfully.

Affected runtime behavior is verified.

No critical regression exists.

The final engineering report is produced.

---

End of Article 01.
